title: Incorrect divisibility claim in forward direction

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
In the proof of Lemma 1 (forward direction), the proof states: "By definition [G:П‡^O(G)] is the maximal [G:H] with G/H admissible (and all others divide it)". The parenthetical "all others divide it" is a divisibility claim вЂ” that [G:H] | [G:П‡^O(G)] for every admissible H вЂ” and this is false in general. Counterexample: take G = S_3 (symmetric group), with П‡^O(S_3) = Z/2 (order 2), so [G:П‡^O(G)] = 3. If Z/3 is also admissible (i.e., Z/3 в†’ S_3), then [G:Z/3] = 2, and 2 does not divide 3.

What is actually used вЂ” and what is actually true вЂ” is only the inequality [G:H] в‰¤ [G:П‡^O(G)] for all admissible H (since П‡^O(G) вЉ† H for all H в†’ G, so |П‡^O(G)| в‰¤ |H|, hence [G:П‡^O(G)] в‰Ґ [G:H]). This inequality is sufficient to establish:

  [G:П‡^O(G)] В· ОЈ_H n_H в‰Ґ ОЈ_H n_H [G:H] в‰Ґ n.

The divisibility claim should be removed as it is both wrong and unnecessary.

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Correction to this issue (critic-agent)

On re-examination, the divisibility claim in the solution is in fact **correct**, and the S_3 counterexample given here is **invalid**.

**The divisibility is true.** П‡^O(G) = в‹‚_{Kв†’G} K вЉ† H for every admissible H, so П‡^O(G) is a subgroup of H, and by Lagrange |П‡^O(G)| divides |H|. Then
  [G:П‡^O(G)] / [G:H] = (|G|/|П‡^O(G)|)/(|G|/|H|) = |H|/|П‡^O(G)| в€€ в„¤,
so [G:H] | [G:П‡^O(G)] for every admissible H.

**The S_3 counterexample is impossible.** The premise "П‡^O(S_3)=в„¤/2 and в„¤/3 admissible" violates the transfer-system axioms: if в„¤/3 в†’ S_3 and в„¤/2 в†’ S_3, then closure under restriction (with J=в„¤/2 вЉ† S_3) gives в„¤/3 в€© в„¤/2 = {e} в†’ в„¤/2, and transitivity gives {e} в†’ S_3, forcing П‡^O(S_3)={e}, not в„¤/2. So one cannot simultaneously have П‡=в„¤/2 and в„¤/3 admissible.

**Residual valid point.** The parenthetical is harmless but unnecessary: the forward argument only needs the inequality [G:H] в‰¤ [G:П‡^O(G)]. So this issue should be downgraded from "proof-gap / incorrect claim" to at most a stylistic remark. I do not consider it a genuine mathematical error in the solution.

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Re-review: addressed in current `solution.tex`.
The divisibility claim is gone (string 'divid'/'divis' does not occur in the file). The forward direction now uses only the inequality $|T|\le r_H|T/H|$ via `lem:index`(2)-(3) ($K\to H\Rightarrow\chi(H)\subseteq K\Rightarrow [H:K]\le r_H$). This gap is closed.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
## Resolution (solver-agent)

I reviewed the current `solution.tex` against this issue. The flawed parenthetical "all others divide it" has been **completely removed** (the strings `divid`/`divis` no longer appear), and the forward direction has been refactored so it depends on a clean, separately-proved lemma rather than any divisibility claim.

**Where the divisibility used to live, and what replaced it.** The forward inequality is now isolated in `Lemma~\ref{lem:index}` ("the admissible index"). Setting $r_H:=[H:\chi^{\mathcal O}(H)]$:
- (2): For every single-orbit admissible $H/K\in\mathcal O(H)$, since $K\to H$ forces $\chi^{\mathcal O}(H)\subseteq K$ by minimality, one gets the **inequality** $[H:K]\le r_H$.
- (3): Hence for every admissible $T\in\mathcal O(H)$, $|T|=\sum_i[H:K_i]\le r_H\cdot|T/H|$.

**Where it is used.** Step 3 of the proof of `Lemma~\ref{lem:forward}` invokes only `lem:index`(3): for the top-group generator $N^TS^1$ with $T\in\mathcal O(G)$, $|T|\ge n$, Proposition `prop:phiNT` gives $\mathrm{conn}(\Phi^G N^TS^1)=|T/G|$, and then
$$ r_G\cdot|T/G|\ \ge\ |T|\ \ge\ n. $$
This is exactly the inequality the critic correctly identified as the only thing needed. No divisibility ($[G:H]\mid[G:\chi^{\mathcal O}(G)]$) is asserted or used anywhere.

**On the side debate in the comments.** As noted in the second critic comment, the divisibility statement happens to be *true* (Lagrange: $\chi^{\mathcal O}(H)\subseteq K\subseteq H$ gives $|H/K|\mid r_H$), and the original $S_3$ "counterexample" was inconsistent with the transfer-system axioms (restriction + transitivity would force $\chi^{\mathcal O}(S_3)=\{e\}$). But this is moot: the rewritten proof relies on the inequality alone, which is unconditionally valid, so the proof is robust either way.

No further edits to the proof were required. The gap is closed.
